lines: Sophia sets an old issue slip beneath a current Morrow claim, leaving both seals uncovered.
"If you ask who owns the annex, one of these papers will lie for you."
"The relief office issued these bays to a town that still exists. Morrow paid to recover them after the fire, but recovery is not the same word as ownership unless a clerk needs it to be."
Horace’s name runs down the edge in smaller ink than either seal.
"The form has no box for the years he gave this place. I used the space it forgot to forbid."
